zh.lifeSummary: 當William Davis於1620年出生在英國格盧斯特斯爾斯特拉頓時，他的父親William Davis30歲，母親Martha Wakeman29歲。他於大約1639年與Elizabeth Chamberlain在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼素福爾可洛波利結婚。他們至少是9個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼洛波利住了大約15年。他去世於1683年12月9日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼素福爾可波斯頓享年63歲，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼素福爾可波斯頓金斯 查阿培爾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当William Davis于1620年出生在英国格盧斯特斯爾斯特拉頓时，他的父亲William Davis30岁，母亲Martha Wakeman29岁。他于大约1639年与Elizabeth Chamberlain在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼素福爾可洛波利结婚。他们至少是9个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼洛波利住了大约15年。他去世于1683年12月9日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼素福爾可波斯頓享年63岁，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼素福爾可波斯頓金斯 查阿培爾。

nameMeanings.name: Davis
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Welsh: patronymic meaning ‘Dafydd's (son)’, equivalent to Welsh ap Dafydd, the Welsh form of David . The spelling Davis is more typical in southwestern England northwards as far as Lancashire, where the frequency of the surname largely reflects Welsh migration, but may sometimes represent a native English surname based on Davy (compare Davies ). Davis (including in the sense 2 below) is the eighth most frequent surname in the US. It is also very common among African Americans.
Irish and Scottish: adopted for Gaelic Mac Daibhéid ‘son of David’; see McDevitt . Compare Davies .

History: John Davis or Davys (c. 1550–1605) was an English navigator who searched for the Northwest Passage. — By the 18th century there were numerous persons named Davis in America, including the jurist John Davis, born in 1761 in Plymouth, MA, and Henry Davis, a clergyman and college president, who was born in 1771 in East Hampton, NY. — Jefferson Davis, born in 1808 in KY, was president of the Confederate States of America from 1861 to 1865.

variants.name: David
meanings: Jewish, Welsh, Scottish, English, French, Breton, Portuguese, Czech, Croatian, and Slovenian; Hungarian (Dávid), Slovak (mainly Dávid): from the Hebrew personal name David (in Hungarian and Slovak spelled Dávid), interpreted as ‘beloved’. The name has been perennially popular among Jews, in honor of the Biblical king of this name. His prominence, and the vivid narrative of his life contained in the First Book of Samuel, led to adoption of the name among Christians in the Middle Ages in various parts of Europe. In Britain, the popularity of this as a personal name was increased for two reasons. Firstly by virtue of its being the name of the patron saint of Wales who was abbot-bishop in the 6th century at what became known as Saint David's in Pembrokeshire. There are numerous dedications and placenames honouring the saint in south Wales, and it is no coincidence that the modern surname is heavily concentrated there, especially in Glamorgan. Secondly, the name was borne by two kings of Scotland (David I, reigning 1124–53, and David II, 1329–71). Its popularity in Russia is largely due to the fact that this was the ecclesiastical name adopted by Saint Gleb (died 1015), one of two sons of Prince Vladimir of Kiev who were martyred for their Christian zeal. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from some other languages, especially Assyrian/Chaldean Dawid and Arabic Daud (with variants, such as Daoud and Dawood ).

History: Guillaume David from France married Marie Armand in Trois-Rivières, QC, in 1656. — This surname is listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of America.

variants.name: Davies
meanings: Welsh and English: variant of Davis , a patronymic equivalent to Welsh ap Dafydd ‘Dafydd's son’. The name is especially numerous in south Wales. This form of the name probably shows the influence of Davy , the common English form of the personal name David in the late medieval and early modern periods (see Davey ).

variants.name: Daves
meanings: Welsh and English: probably a variant of   Davis   or   Davies  .
In some cases also German (Däves): from a shortened form of the personal name Matthäus (see Matthew ). This surname is very rare in Germany.
In some cases probably also an Americanized form of German   Tewes  , a cognate of 2 above.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When William Davis was born in 1620, in Stratton, Gloucestershire, England, his father, William Davis, was 30 and his mother, Martha Wakeman, was 29. He married Elizabeth Chamberlain about 1639, in Roxbury, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. They were the parents of at least 9 sons and 3 daughters. He lived in Roxbury, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America for about 15 years. He died on 9 December 1683, in Boston, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, at the age of 63, and was buried in Kings Chapel, Boston, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America.

it.lifeSummary: Quando William Davis è nato il nel 1620 nel Stratton, Gloucestershire, England, il suo padre, William Davis, aveva 30 anni e il suo madre, Martha Wakeman, aveva 29 anni. Lui sposato Elizabeth Chamberlain circa 1639, nel Roxbury, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. Erano genitori di almeno 9 figli e 3 figlie. Vissuto a Roxbury, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America per circa 15 anni. Morì il 9 dicembre 1683, nel Boston, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, all'età di 63 anni, e fu sepolto a Kings Chapel, Boston, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America.

ja.lifeSummary: William Davisが1620年にイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアストラットオンで生まれた時，父William Davisは30歳，母Martha Wakemanは29歳であった。約1639年にイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニースフォルクロクスブリーでElizabeth Chamberlainと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも9人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼はイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニーロクスブリーに約15年間住んでいた。彼は1683年12月9日にイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニースフォルクボストンで63歳で死去し，イギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニースフォルクボストンキングズ チャペルにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: William Davis(이)가 1620년 영국 그루스스터실르 스트래턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William Davis(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Martha Wakeman(은)는 29세였다. 그는 대략 1639년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 수폴크 록스버이에서 Elizabeth Chamberlain(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 아홉 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 록스버이에서 약 15년 동안 살았다. 그는 1683년 12월 9일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 수폴크 보스턴에서 63세의 나이로 사망했으며 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 수폴크 보스턴 킨그스 차플에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando William Davis nasceu em 1620, em Stratton, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, seu pai, William Davis, tinha 30 anos e sua mãe, Martha Wakeman, tinha 29 anos. Ele casou-se com Elizabeth Chamberlain aproximadamente 1639, em Roxbury, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. Eles tiveram pelo menos 9 filhos e 3 filhas. Ele viveu em Roxbury, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America por quase 15 anos. Ele faleceu em 9 de dezembro de 1683, em Boston, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, com 63 anos, e foi sepultado em Kings Chapel, Boston, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William Davis родился в 1620 г. в местности страттон, глоусестиршир, Англия, его отцу по имени William Davis было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Martha Wakeman, 29. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth Chamberlain около 1639 г. в местности роксбури, суффолк, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика. У них было по крайней мере 9 сыновей и 3 дочери. Он жил в местности роксбури, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика около 15 лет. Он умер 9 декабря 1683 г. в местности бостон, суффолк, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 63 лет и был похоронен в местности кингс чапел, бостон, суффолк, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando William Davis nació en 1620, en Stratton, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, su papá, William Davis, tenía 30 años y su mamá, Martha Wakeman, tenía 29 años. Se casó con Elizabeth Chamberlain aproximadamente 1639, en Roxbury, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. Fueron padres de por lo menos 9 hijos y 3 hijas. Vivió en Roxbury, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America durante unos 15 años. Murió el 9 de diciembre de 1683, en Boston, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, a la edad de 63 años, y fue sepultado en Kings Chapel, Boston, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America.
